summary: Round 8 closed as a perfect 8 from 8 tipping week with no charity bet placed, leaving the Mark Hughes Foundation bank unchanged at $6.50 while the board waits for Round 9 team lists.

scoreboard.note: The Tigers got the job done to start the week and kept the perfect-round card alive.
Cronulla justified the cleaner pre-game read and kept the card moving.
Canterbury landed as expected and validated the no-bet discipline around a short price.
The Roosters handled the spot and kept the clean sweep on track.
The Warriors held serve at home despite the CNK absence concern.
Souths rewarded the trust call and kept Melbourne in the prove-it bin.
Penrith still had too much control and class for Newcastle.
Manly held together without Turbo and completed the perfect 8 from 8 slate.

liveAlerts.text: Round 8 landed as a perfect 8 from 8 tipping week, which is worth logging cleanly before thinking about Round 9.
No charity bet was placed in Round 8, so the Mark Hughes Foundation bank stays at $6.50.
The cleanest win of the week may have been the process itself: no forced bet, no damage to the bank.
Round 9 should not be front-run until fresh team lists land.
The Tigers Round 7 miss remains the last bet in the ledger, so the next play deserves patience.
Today is for wrapping the 8 from 8 and carrying the bank forward unchanged.
